To derive the total query response time function $ T_{\text{Total}}(Q) $ we must consider the individual response time functions of each database and the proportion of queries they handle. The total response time is the sum of the response times of each database based on the number of queries it processes. 

The number of queries handled by each database is determined by the proportion $ \alpha $ for MySQL and $ \beta $ for Oracle with Sybase handling the remaining $ (1 - \alpha - \beta)Q $ queries. 

The response time for each database is calculated using their respective functions. For MySQL the response time is based on the number of queries $ \alpha Q $. For Oracle the response time is based on $ \beta Q $. For Sybase the response time is based on $ (1 - \alpha - \beta)Q $.

The total query response time is the sum of the response times of all three databases. 

The total query response time function $ T_{\text{Total}}(Q) $ is the sum of the response times of each database.
